mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for users looking to deploy LLMs with minimal latency and resource consumption. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of managing model configurations.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM Helm chart in the specified namespace, setting up the necessary resources for vLLM to function.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Installs vLLM in a new namespace called 'vllm'.

steps.title: Create a Custom Resource for vLLM
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) that defines the configuration for your vLLM deployment, including model specifications.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This CR specifies a deployment of the 'gpt-3' model with 2 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure that it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Lists all pods in the vllm namespace to verify that they are running.
> Checks the status of the vLLM custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM custom resource.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> Uninstalls the vLLM Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This process will upgrade vLLM to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Backs up the current vLLM configuration.
> Upgrades vLLM to version 0.18.0.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace may not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the vLLM Helm chart is installed and the namespace is created.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM deployment may be misconfigured or the specified model may not be available.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits on your nodes or scale down the number of replicas.

troubleshooting.title: Custom Resource not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Custom Resource definition using 'kubectl apply -f <your-cr-file.yaml>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory efficiency.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n vllm'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
